data.title: CareTrust REIT's UK acquisition sets stage for diversified growth
data.description:
CareTrust REIT's (NYSE:CTRE) $817M acquisition of Care REIT, its first in the U.K., last month provides a platform for the healthcare REIT to boost its growth and diversify its operations, CEO Dave Sedgwick said on Wednesday.

While the company had hesitated about making an acquisition overseas because its U.S. business was growing so well, management finally came to the realization that that is when it's best to diversify. 

"Because it ain’t broke, we have the opportunity to do something really extraordinary," Sedgwick said at Nareit's REITweek conference in New York. That gave the REIT the ability to look at the acquisition for the long run.  "It's a diversification play that's really helpful," he added. If its U.S. operations sputter, then it has another avenue of growth.

The acquisition gives CareTrust (NYSE:CTRE) a team in the U.K. to build up a business there. The company expects to internalize the UK team by the end of the month. "There are no guarantees, but I think we're on a good track to do that," Sedgwick said.

In addition to the long-run prospects, the current economics of the deal were attractive. So much so that CTRE raised its guidance range to normalized FFO per share of $1.75-$1.78 from $1.68-$1.72 before the acquisition. It expects cost synergies of the deal at ~$5M.

"We really like this opportunity set in front of us," Chief Investment Officer James Callister said. "There's a really large pool of excellent operators who haven't had the access to capital — operators that really want to grow. It sets up another pipeline of growth. If anything sputters here, we have a new source of growth over there."

As for CareTrust's (CTRE) nearer term prospects, "we like the current deal flow," Callister said. "Right now it’s predominantly in skilled nursing" and in the U.S. "We’re in a great position to execute on those transactions that work for us," he said.

Another area the company, which has traditionally operated in the triple net lease segment, is looking at is the senior housing operating portfolio ("SHOP") segment. "We are actively looking at SHOP," Sedgwick said.  And the company has come close to a deal. 

He points to three strengths the company has — its focus on growth, its strong balance sheet, and its investment team, "which has done a fantastic job," he said.

CareTrust (CTRE) stock rose 1.1% in midafternoon trading.

data.title: Equity REITs more than double stock buybacks in Q1 - report
data.description:
Equity REITs more than doubled their stock repurchase activity in the first quarter, with the sector buying back about $993.2M of shares, an analysis by S&P Global Market Intelligence showed.

Comparatively, the sector repurchased $456.2M worth of shares in the fourth quarter of 2024 and $859.8M in the first quarter of 2024, the report said.

The analysis takes into account equity REITs traded on the Nasdaq, NYSE, or NYSE American.

Buybacks by Alexandria Real Estate Equities (ARE) is said to have reached the highest value. The health care REIT repurchased $212.9M worth of stocks during the first quarter.

Office REIT JBG SMITH Properties (JBGS) followed with repurchases worth $187.5M.

Among the subsectors, hotel REITs reportedly were the biggest repurchasers. The subsector saw buybacks worth $285.3M during the first quarter.

Office followed with $223.6M in share repurchases.

The equity REITs sector saw 13 new share repurchase programs announced during the first quarter, according to Market Intelligence.

Retail REIT Realty Income (O) announced the largest new buyback plan of up to $2B, the report noted.

data.title: Yields slide as soft jobs data and debt concerns spur bond buying
data.description:
U.S. Treasury yields fell sharply on Wednesday as investors moved into government bonds following a weaker than expected ADP jobs report and fresh concerns over national debt levels.

The benchmark U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) dropped 10 basis points to 4.35%, its lowest level since May 9. The U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y), which is more sensitive to interest rate expectations, also declined 9 basis points to 3.87%, matching its lowest mark in nearly a month. 

The pullback in yields came after ADP data showed U.S. private sector employment increased by just 37K jobs in May, far short of economists’ forecasts for a 110K gain. The figure also marked a slowdown from April’s revised 60K increase, signaling potential softening in the labor market and fueling expectations the Federal Reserve may stay on hold or pivot to rate cuts sooner than anticipated. 

Market sentiment was further dampened by a report from the Congressional Budget Office, which estimated that a GOP-backed bill aligned with President Donald Trump's policy agenda would add $2.4T to the national debt over the next decade. 

Equity markets remained largely flat amid the bond rally, with investors weighing softer economic data against mounting fiscal concerns.

Treasury and Fixed Income ETFs: (NASDAQ:TLT), (NYSEARCA:TLH), (NASDAQ:IEF), (NASDAQ:IEI), (NASDAQ:SHY), (NYSEARCA:SGOV), (NYSEARCA:SCHO), (NYSEARCA:BIL), (NYSEARCA:AGG), (NASDAQ:BND), (NASDAQ:VCIT), (NYSEARCA:MUB), (NASDAQ:MBB), (NYSEARCA:JNK), (NYSEARCA:LQD), (NYSEARCA:HYG), and (NYSEARCA:TIP).

data.title: Stocks inch up, Treasury yields slide as traders snap up bonds after weak data
data.description:
Wall Street on Wednesday was clinging on to slight gains, while bonds rallied after private hiring in May reached its lowest level in over two years.

U.S. indices picked up from the flat line in afternoon trade. The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last +0.2%, the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.3%, and the Dow (DJI) was +0.1%.

Private sector employment grew by 37K in May, missing the +110K consensus and cooling from the 62K increase in April, according to data released by ADP. 

Market participants reacted to the data by snapping up bonds on rising expectations that the Federal Reserve will cut interest rates, sending U.S. Treasury yields lower. The 10-year yield (US10Y) was down 11 basis points to 4.36%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was down 8 basis points to 3.88%.

Donald Trump took to Truth Social to once again express frustration at Fed chair Jerome Powell. "'Too Late' Powell must now LOWER THE RATE. He is unbelievable!!! Europe has lowered NINE TIMES!" the President said. 

Trump also said Chinese President Xi Jinping has been “extremely hard” to strike a deal with, as the White House hinted at the possibility of the two leaders speaking this week amid escalating trade tensions.

"Many investors continue to look past the current trade confusion, convinced that it will all turn out fine in the end. And as things stand, nothing much should happen between now and July to upset markets, as it is next month when the high reciprocal tariffs would kick in, assuming the absence of any bilateral trade deals, while there also around six weeks until the start of the second quarter earnings season," David Morrison, senior market analyst at Trade Nation 

U.S. major averages ended Tuesday with solid gains as encouraging job openings data overshadowed a major intergovernmental organization downgrading its global and U.S. economic growth projections. 

One of the most important catalysts for the recovery was the JOLTS report, which showed the US labor market in a more robust position than previously thought, Reid added. 

The U.S. ISM Services PMI retreated to 49.9 in May from 51.6 in April.

Furthermore, the S&P Global U.S. Composite PMI advanced to 53.0 in May's final estimate, compared with the 52.1 flash reading and 50.6 in April.

data.title: OpenAI surpasses 3M paying business subscribers
data.description:
• OpenAI, the generative artificial intelligence company backed by Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), said on a livestream on Wednesday that it has surpassed 3M paying business subscribers, up from 2M paying users in February.
  • The figure includes enterprises that use ChatGPT Enterprise, ChatGPT Team and ChatGPT Edu customers, OpenAI added.
  • During the livestream, the company also announced it has ChatGPT, OpenAI's generative AI chatbot, will be able to access “connectors,” including Google Drive, Dropbox and more, without leaving ChatGPT.
  • In April, OpenAI CEO Sam Altman said that more than 800M people were using ChatGPT, a doubling that occurred in “just a few weeks.”
  • OpenAI is now the world's most valuable startup, worth $300B as of its last funding round. Its valuation has nearly doubled since October 2024.

data.title: Chagee rallies after Citi starts off coverage with a bullish view
data.description:
Chinese tea chain Chagee (NASDAQ:CHA) landed its second bull rating on Wednesday after the IPO quiet period on the stock expired.

Citi started off coverage with a Buy rating and price target of $43.70, representing upside potential of more than 30%. 

Analyst Xiaopo Wei said Chagee is a fast-growing premium tea play, noting the company's roughly 20% market share among China's premium freshly made tea brands. Wei expects Chagee (NASDAQ:CHA) to continue to grow rapidly abroad as it leverages its automation and standardization capabilities. 

"As the leader in the premium segment, Chagee well differentiates itself from most value players with its solid long-term product strategy (simple core menu), managed franchise model (with technology-powered Five Things Online), strong brand equity (via social-media marketing), and high efficiency (via automation and standardization)," highlighted Wei.

Morgan Stanley, which also has a bull rating on Chagee (CHA), weighed in on the company as well. "Our channel checks and our AlphaWise consumer survey suggest that Chagee's (CHA) store operations (payback period and margins) are still relatively healthy and at competitive levels compared to peers, and that it continues to hold strong brand preference among consumers," updated analyst Lillian Lou and team.

At the end of Q1, Chagee (CHA) operated a total of 6,681 teahouses in Greater China and overseas, representing 64% growth in the number of teahouses from March 31, 2024.

Chagee (CHA) is a competitor with Luckin Coffee (OTCPK:LKNCY) and Starbucks (SBUX) in China and recently opened its first U.S. location.

Shares of Chagee (CHA) moved 6.5% higher in afternoon trading to $32.40. The IPO was priced at $28 per share.

data.title: Inotiv discloses completion of SEC investigation
data.description:
Inotiv (NASDAQ:NOTV) said on Wednesday that the U.S. SEC's Division of Enforcement finished its investigation into the company related to importation of non-human primates from Asia.

The company had previously disclosed that on May 23, 2023, it received a voluntary request from the SEC for documents related to its involvement, alongside Envigo Global Services and Orient BioResource Center, in the importation of non-human primates from Asia.

Post that, the SEC launched an investigation into the matter, which the company disclosed is now complete.

Based on the information received by the company, the SEC does not plan to suggest any enforcement action against Inotiv.

data.title: Mexico regulator expected to approve US Steel-Nippon tie-up - Reuters
data.description:
Mexico's antitrust regulator is expected to renew its approval of Nippon Steel's (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) bid for US Steel (NYSE:X) as soon as tomorrow, removing one of the last remaining hurdles to the tie-up, Reuters reported Wednesday.

The Cofece regulator previously approved the merger, but Mexican approval of the proposed acquisition, first announced in December 2023, has since expired and must be renewed for the merger to move forward.

US Steel (NYSE:X) shares are little changed after President Trump signed his order to double tariffs on steel and aluminum imports to 50%, as expected.

data.title: Cleveland-Cliffs scraps $500M hydrogen-based Ohio steel project
data.description:
Cleveland-Cliffs (NYSE:CLF) said Tuesday it has officially abandoned its $500M hydrogen-based steel project in Middletown, Ohio, due to concerns over insufficient clean hydrogen supply, shifting focus to extend the life of its coal-fired blast furnace instead.

CEO Lourenco Goncalves cited delays in hydrogen production and pending Trump administration policies as key reasons for the decision, despite earlier support from a $500M U.S. Department of Energy grant under former President Biden that would have replaced the coal-based blast furnace with a hydrogen-fueled plant.

Goncalves said that even with the grant, Cleveland-Cliffs (NYSE:CLF) would need to spend an additional $1.1B, bringing the total project cost to $1.6B, but the company is in discussions with the Trump administration to renegotiate the grant to align with new energy priorities.

"Without hydrogen, the entire thing falls apart," Goncalves said at an event hosted by the American Iron and Steel Institute. "At the very least, I will not have hydrogen at the time I need for that specific project."

The CEO also said the U.S. Federal Reserve is holding down demand for steel used in construction and manufacturing by not cutting interest rates.

"The Fed is ridiculously late in acting," Goncalves said. "For the steel industry, it’s killing steel consumption."

data.title: Cogent reports insider transactions worth over $3M
data.description:
Cogent Communications (NASDAQ:CCOI) said in an SEC filing that CEO Dave Schaeffer sold stake worth more than $3M in the internet access and private network services provider.

Schaeffer sold 65K shares of common stock in two tranches at $46.3827 per share and $46.1337 per share.
